I agree that surgical intervention represented by option D (ESWL or another form of lithotripsy) would be the preferred approach here. Given the large size of the kidney stone (2.5 cm), its presence in the renal pelvis, and the associated symptoms including severe colic, conservative management alone may not adequately address the situation quickly enough to avoid complications such as worsening obstruction, infection, or even loss of renal function. Additionally, considering the high success rates and lower invasiveness compared to open surgery methods listed under other options like pyelolithotomy or nephroureterostomy, ESWL remains an excellent first-line therapeutic modality for pediatric patients presenting similarly.
